title: Reach for PEFT before full fine-tuning
description: Try parameter-efficient methods like LoRA before updating all weights, since they cost far less and forget less of the base.
body: Use when adapting a large pretrained model to a task with limited compute. Do not full-fine-tune by default — updating all weights is expensive and overwrites general capabilities. Actions: start with LoRA/PEFT on attention and MLP projections, sweep rank and learning rate, and compare against the base before considering full tuning. Validation signals include a LoRA run that matches or beats full fine-tuning on the task eval at a fraction of the cost, and a capability holdout that holds. Risk boundary: full fine-tuning a frontier-scale model risks catastrophic forgetting and large spend; if PEFT meets the target, do not escalate to full tuning without explicit justification.
